 {"id":"bd-1afu6","title":"Feature: Cross-Project Ensemble Sharing","description":"## Summary
Export and import ensemble configurations across projects with validation and safety controls.

## Rationale
Sharing ensembles spreads best practices and reduces setup time.

## Implementation
- Export to a versioned, self-contained file (TOML/JSON) with schema version.
- Import validates:
  - schema version
  - mode IDs exist in catalog
  - synthesis/budget settings are valid
- Remote import is **disabled by default**; requires explicit `--allow-remote` and checksum verification.
- Track imported presets separately and detect version conflicts.

## Logging
- Log import/export paths, validation errors, checksum results.

## Tests
- Unit: schema validation + versioning
- Integration: export→import roundtrip
- E2E: local import; remote import rejected unless `--allow-remote`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Export creates a self-contained config file
- [ ] Import validates and installs safely
- [ ] Remote import requires explicit allow + checksum
- [ ] Version conflicts detected and reported
- [ ] Imported ensembles listed separately

## Dependencies
- Requires ensemble config types (bd-3i16o)
- Requires mode catalog (bd-11mtt)
- Requires built-in ensembles for examples (bd-cph20)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:07:08.692037915Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T21:57:25.208177477Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1afu6","depends_on_id":"bd-11mtt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:57:09.263513914Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1afu6","depends_on_id":"bd-1h4ox","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:11:04.881245046Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1afu6","depends_on_id":"bd-3i16o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:14:06.397478371Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1afu6","depends_on_id":"bd-cph20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:57:25.208152289Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1alai","title":"Unit Tests: Robot Diagnose","description":"Unit tests for Robot Diagnose command.

## Test Coverage

### Health Classification Tests
- Test healthy detection (recent output, running process)
- Test rate_limited detection (pattern match)
- Test unresponsive detection (old output, running)
- Test crashed detection (no process)
- Test unknown edge cases
- Test edge case: agent in ultrathink (long thinking, not stuck)

### Recommendation Tests
- Test each status maps to correct action
- Test fix_command generation with correct session/pane
- Test reset_time parsing from various formats
- Test auto_fixable assignment logic
- Test recommendation ordering (priority)

### --fix Tests
- Test only auto_fixable items attempted
- Test sequential execution order
- Test partial failure handling (some fixes succeed, some fail)
- Test skip rate_limited items
- Test --fix with empty auto_fixable list

### Summary Tests
- Test pane counting accuracy
- Test overall_health classification thresholds
- Test panes grouping correctness
- Test --brief output format

### --pane Flag Tests
- Test single pane filtering
- Test non-existent pane error
- Test control pane (1) handling
- Test output format with filter

## Mocking Strategy

Mock status enrichment data to simulate:
- Various process states
- Rate limit patterns
- Different output freshness levels
- Mixed health scenarios

## Logging Requirements

All tests must include detailed logging:
- Log test scenario at start
- Log mock data configuration
- Log expected vs actual on each assertion
- Log full diagnose response for debugging
- Use structured JSON logging","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:28:09.444366395Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T06:32:07.028417016Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T06:32:07.028360339Z","close_reason":"Implemented comprehensive unit tests in diagnose_test.go with 25 test cases covering: TestDetermineOverallHealth (13 cases), TestBuildRateLimitRecommendation (3 cases), TestDiagnoseRecommendation_JSONStructure, TestDiagnoseOutput_JSONEnvelope, TestDiagnoseOutput_EmptySlicesNotNil, TestDiagnoseSummary_TotalPanes, TestDiagnoseBriefOutput_JSONStructure, TestDiagnoseOptions, TestRecommendationActions (4 cases), and TestOverallHealthThresholds (3 cases). All tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1alai","depends_on_id":"bd-1pt6v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:39:28.774371521Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1alai","depends_on_id":"bd-26y68","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:28:31.490695307Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1alai","depends_on_id":"bd-31e1f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:28:09.473784786Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1alai","depends_on_id":"bd-3u6dg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:28:44.038995181Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1alai","depends_on_id":"bd-fzhuh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:28:39.711210482Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1alai","depends_on_id":"bd-kk81t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:28:35.439816788Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1av7i","title":"Task: Implement OutputCollector for ensemble outputs","description":"# Task: Implement OutputCollector for ensemble outputs

Collect and normalize outputs from all ensemble agents into a structured SynthesisInput.

## Component Overview
The OutputCollector aggregates per-pane captures, validates them against OutputSchema, and produces a normalized SynthesisInput for downstream synthesis and auditing.

## Implementation Details

### Core Interface
```go
// internal/ensemble/collector.go
type OutputCollector struct {
    SessionName string
    Outputs     map[string]*ModeOutput
    Raw         map[string]string
    Errors      map[string][]error
}

func (c *OutputCollector) Collect(session *EnsembleSession) error
func (c *OutputCollector) CollectWindow(idx int) (*ModeOutput, error)
func (c *OutputCollector) ToSynthesisInput() *SynthesisInput
```

### Collection Strategy
1. Use OutputCapture to fetch raw pane text + parsed YAML
2. Validate ModeOutput via SchemaValidator
3. Attach ModeID/ModeName/ModeCode from assignment metadata
4. **Normalize** with SchemaValidator.Normalize(output, modeID):
   - inject `mode_id` if missing
   - map likelihood/confidence strings (high|medium|low) to floats
5. Normalize into SynthesisInput (deterministic ordering)

### Error Handling
- Partial collection allowed (flagged in SynthesisInput)
- Validation errors recorded per-pane
- Capture errors do not abort entire run

## Logging Requirements
- Log per-pane capture + validation outcomes
- Log injected mode_id occurrences
- Log string-to-float normalization events

## File Location
internal/ensemble/collector.go

## Dependencies
- Requires output capture (bd-1dmet)
- Requires schema validation (bd-1wnxl)
- Feeds synthesis system (bd-2qwm8)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Collects outputs from all panes
- [ ] Handles partial/invalid outputs gracefully
- [ ] Injects `mode_id` when missing
- [ ] Maps string likelihood/confidence values to floats
- [ ] Produces deterministic SynthesisInput
- [ ] Logs per-pane capture + validation outcomes","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:39:50.102233668Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T18:07:59.499524336Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1av7i","depends_on_id":"bd-1dmet","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:51:17.140199802Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1av7i","depends_on_id":"bd-1wnxl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T09:58:08.947244332Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1av7i","depends_on_id":"bd-2qwm8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T09:31:09.437231574Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1av7i","title":"Task: Implement OutputCollector for ensemble outputs","description":"# Task: Implement OutputCollector for ensemble outputs

Collect and normalize outputs from all ensemble agents into a structured SynthesisInput.

## Component Overview
The OutputCollector aggregates per-pane captures, validates them against OutputSchema, and produces a normalized SynthesisInput for downstream synthesis and auditing.

## Implementation Details

### Core Interface
```go
// internal/ensemble/collector.go
type OutputCollector struct {
    SessionName string
    Outputs     map[string]*ModeOutput
    Raw         map[string]string
    Errors      map[string][]error
}

func (c *OutputCollector) Collect(session *EnsembleSession) error
func (c *OutputCollector) CollectWindow(idx int) (*ModeOutput, error)
func (c *OutputCollector) ToSynthesisInput() *SynthesisInput
```

### Collection Strategy
1. Use OutputCapture to fetch raw pane text + parsed YAML
2. Validate ModeOutput via SchemaValidator
3. Attach ModeID/ModeName/ModeCode from assignment metadata
4. **Normalize** with SchemaValidator.Normalize(output, modeID):
   - inject `mode_id` if missing
   - map likelihood/confidence strings (high|medium|low) to floats
5. Normalize into SynthesisInput (deterministic ordering)

### Error Handling
- Partial collection allowed (flagged in SynthesisInput)
- Validation errors recorded per-pane
- Capture errors do not abort entire run

## Logging Requirements
- Log per-pane capture + validation outcomes
- Log injected mode_id occurrences
- Log string-to-float normalization events

## File Location
internal/ensemble/collector.go

## Dependencies
- Requires output capture (bd-1dmet)
- Requires schema validation (bd-1wnxl)
- Feeds synthesis system (bd-2qwm8)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Collects outputs from all panes
- [ ] Handles partial/invalid outputs gracefully
- [ ] Injects `mode_id` when missing
- [ ] Maps string likelihood/confidence values to floats
- [ ] Produces deterministic SynthesisInput
- [ ] Logs per-pane capture + validation outcomes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:39:50.102233668Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T15:26:24.179400709Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T15:26:24.179372626Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1av7i","depends_on_id":"bd-1dmet","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:51:17.140199802Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1av7i","depends_on_id":"bd-1wnxl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T09:58:08.947244332Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1av7i","depends_on_id":"bd-2qwm8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T09:31:09.437231574Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ay2","title":"Use lipgloss.Width() for all styled string width calculations","description":"## Problem
Width calculations using len() or utf8.RuneCountInString() on styled strings include ANSI escape codes, causing misalignment.

## Code Locations to Audit
- internal/cli/table.go - Table rendering with runeWidth(), stripANSI()
- internal/tui/layout/layout.go - TruncateRunes() function
- internal/tui/styles/styles.go - Gradient/shimmer calculations
- internal/tui/dashboard/dashboard.go - Various width calculations
- internal/tui/components/help.go - Already correct (uses lipgloss.Width)

## Current Issues
1. **table.go:runeWidth()** - Custom function may not handle ANSI correctly
2. **table.go:stripANSI()** - Stripping then measuring is inefficient vs lipgloss.Width
3. **TruncateRunes()** - May truncate mid-ANSI sequence

## Fix Pattern
\`\`\`go
// BEFORE: Various approaches
width := len(styledText)                    // Includes ANSI bytes
width := utf8.RuneCountInString(styledText) // Includes ANSI runes
width := runeWidth(stripANSI(text))         // Extra allocation + regex

// AFTER: Consistent approach
width := lipgloss.Width(styledText)         // Visual width only

// For truncation
func TruncateSmart(s string, maxWidth int) string {
    if lipgloss.Width(s) <= maxWidth {
        return s
    }
    // Use ansi-aware truncation
    return truncate.StringWithTail(s, uint(maxWidth), "…")
}
\`\`\`

## Files to Modify

### internal/cli/table.go
Replace runeWidth/stripANSI with lipgloss.Width:
\`\`\`go
// BEFORE
func (t *TableWriter) calculateColumnWidths() {
    for _, row := range t.rows {
        for i, cell := range row {
            w := runeWidth(stripANSI(cell))
            // ...
        }
    }
}

// AFTER
func (t *TableWriter) calculateColumnWidths() {
    for _, row := range t.rows {
        for i, cell := range row {
            w := lipgloss.Width(cell)
            // ...
        }
    }
}
\`\`\`

### internal/tui/layout/layout.go
\`\`\`go
// BEFORE
func TruncateRunes(s string, max int) string {
    runes := []rune(s)
    if len(runes) > max {
        return string(runes[:max-1]) + "…"
    }
    return s
}

// AFTER
func TruncateToWidth(s string, maxWidth int) string {
    if lipgloss.Width(s) <= maxWidth {
        return s
    }
    return truncate.StringWithTail(s, uint(maxWidth), "…")
}
\`\`\`

## Dependencies
Add to go.mod if not present:
\`\`\`
github.com/muesli/truncate
github.com/mattn/go-runewidth (for non-lipgloss contexts)
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All width calculations use lipgloss.Width() or equivalent
- [ ] Tables with styled cells align correctly
- [ ] Truncation doesn't break ANSI sequences
- [ ] Emoji and CJK characters measured correctly

## Unit Tests
1. TestWidth_StyledString - lipgloss.Width gives visual width
2. TestWidth_Emoji - 🎉 counts as 2 cells
3. TestWidth_CJK - 日本語 counts as 6 cells  
4. TestTruncate_PreservesANSI - truncated string still valid ANSI
5. TestTable_StyledCellAlignment - columns align with styled content

## Verification
\`\`\`go
func TestWidth_Examples(t *testing.T) {
    cases := []struct {
        input string
        want  int
    }{
        {"hello", 5},
        {lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true).Render("hello"), 5},
        {"🎉", 2},
        {"日本語", 6},
        {"\x1b[31mred\x1b[0m", 3},
    }
    for _, tc := range cases {
        got := lipgloss.Width(tc.input)
        if got != tc.want {
            t.Errorf("Width(%q) = %d, want %d", tc.input, got, tc.want)
        }
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Logging
- DEBUG: Log width calculations with input length vs visual width
- WARN: Log when width calculation fallback used","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:11:43.092942693Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T08:55:06.350443242Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T08:55:06.350359103Z","close_reason":"All acceptance criteria met: table.go runeWidth() uses lipgloss.Width(), styles.go uses lipgloss.Width() for all visual width calculations, layout.go has TruncateWidth() using lipgloss.Width(). Tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ay2","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:17:25.813556494Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1azv5","title":"Task: Implement ContextPackGenerator for auto-generating project briefs","description":"# Task: ContextPackGenerator Implementation

Auto-generate project context from existing artifacts.

## Implementation
```go
// internal/ensemble/context_generator.go
type ContextPackGenerator struct {
    ProjectRoot string
    GitClient   GitClient
    BeadsClient BeadsClient
}

type ContextPack struct {
    ProjectBrief ProjectBrief
    UserContext  UserContext
    Hash         string    // For cache invalidation
    GeneratedAt  time.Time
}

type ProjectBrief struct {
    Name           string
    Description    string   // First paragraph of README
    Languages      []string // Detected from file extensions
    Frameworks     []string // From package.json, go.mod, etc.
    EntryPoints    []string
    CorePackages   []string
    TestCoverage   float64
    RecentCommits  []CommitSummary
    OpenIssueCount int
}

func NewContextPackGenerator(root string) *ContextPackGenerator
func (g *ContextPackGenerator) Generate() (*ContextPack, error)
func (g *ContextPackGenerator) DetectLanguages() []string
func (g *ContextPackGenerator) DetectFrameworks() []string
func (g *ContextPackGenerator) ParseReadme() (string, error)
func (g *ContextPackGenerator) GetRecentCommits(n int) ([]CommitSummary, error)
func (g *ContextPackGenerator) CountOpenIssues() (int, error)
```

## Detection Logic
- Languages: Count file extensions in src/
- Frameworks: Parse go.mod, package.json, Cargo.toml, requirements.txt
- Entry points: main.go, index.ts, main.py, etc.
- Core packages: Top-level directories under src/ or internal/

 {"id":"bd-1b9f","title":"Unit Tests: Round-robin distribution algorithm","description":"## Test Coverage
Unit tests for the round-robin distribution algorithm.

## Test Cases

### Basic Distribution
```go
func TestRoundRobinDistribution(t *testing.T) {
    agents := []string{"cc_1", "cc_2", "cod_1"}
    beads := []string{"b1", "b2", "b3", "b4", "b5", "b6"}

    result := RoundRobin(agents, beads)

    // Expected: cc_1=[b1,b4], cc_2=[b2,b5], cod_1=[b3,b6]
    assert.Equal(t, []string{"b1", "b4"}, result["cc_1"])
    assert.Equal(t, []string{"b2", "b5"}, result["cc_2"])
    assert.Equal(t, []string{"b3", "b6"}, result["cod_1"])
}
```

### Edge Cases
- Empty agents list -> error
- Empty beads list -> empty result
- Single agent -> all beads to one agent
- More agents than beads -> some agents get nothing
- Uneven distribution -> remainder spread

### Logging Requirements
```go
func TestWithLogging(t *testing.T) {
    t.Logf("[TEST-UNIT-RR] test=%s input_agents=%d input_beads=%d",
        t.Name(), len(agents), len(beads))

    result := RoundRobin(agents, beads)

    for agent, assigned := range result {
        t.Logf("[TEST-UNIT-RR] agent=%s assigned_count=%d beads=%v",
            agent, len(assigned), assigned)
    }

    t.Logf("[TEST-UNIT-RR] test=%s status=complete total_assigned=%d",
        t.Name(), totalAssigned(result))
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Basic distribution works correctly
- [ ] All edge cases handled
- [ ] Deterministic ordering
- [ ] Verbose logging for debugging
- [ ] Tests run in < 1s","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:21:00.355116409Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T23:22:33.354688322Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T23:22:33.354161951Z","close_reason":"Tests already exist and pass all acceptance criteria. Found 12 comprehensive round-robin tests in internal/assign/matcher_test.go covering: even/uneven distribution, edge cases (empty inputs, single bead, more agents than beads), score/confidence=1.0, deterministic ordering, reason format, bead ordering preservation, and filtering unavailable agents. All tests pass in 0.004s.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1b9f","depends_on_id":"bd-l4y4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:22:31.779226585Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1b9f","depends_on_id":"bd-pjjk7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:37:40.100740064Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
